title: What Do I Want This Decade to Be?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for setting intentions for the decade ahead
excerpt: A set of five journal prompts for intentional stock-taking at a decade boundary. The prompts move from a clear view of where you are now toward a concrete picture of what you want the next ten years to hold.

practice.whenToUse: Use at or near a significant birthday — 50, 55, 60 — or any other moment that prompts a decade-level stock-take.

content.text: A journal prompt set for the decade boundary: not a bucket list, not a productivity review, but a genuine inquiry into what you want the shape of the next ten years to be.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Where am I starting from, in truth?
Write the actual picture: what is working, what is draining, what has been left unattended. This is not a complaint list. It is the baseline from which the decade begins.
What do I want more of in the next ten years?
Not achievement goals. The texture of daily life: what you want to feel, who you want to be spending time with, what you want to be making or doing.
What am I ready to spend less energy on?
The obligations that have run their course, the approval that costs more than it returns, the things you have been maintaining out of habit rather than choice.
What have I been putting off that belongs in this decade?
The thing that has waited long enough. Not the thing you feel you should want. The thing you actually want, that keeps reappearing on the list.
What does this decade look like at its best?
Write a paragraph of what the decade looks like if it goes well. Specific enough to feel real: where you are, who is there, what you are doing, how you feel in the middle of an ordinary Tuesday.
